summary: Joel Spolsky announces the publication of his book 'Smart and Gets Things Done,' a concise guide to finding and hiring the best technical talent. The book compiles and reorganizes material from his Joel on Software blog articles into a short, readable format of about 180 pages. He highlights the illustrations by Terry Colon as a standout feature and walks through the chapter structure covering topics from finding developers to interviewing to fixing teams. The post is essentially a promotional announcement with Joel's characteristic self-deprecating humor about the book's brevity.
key_points: The book distills Joel's hiring and talent management blog posts into a concise, reorganized format
It covers the full hiring pipeline: finding developers, sorting resumes, phone screens, interviewing, and fixing teams
The content originated as Joel on Software articles but was rewritten for book-quality coherence
Joel emphasizes that he writes first and foremost for the web, with books as a secondary format
money_quotes: I write first and foremost for the web, so most of this material originated as Joel on Software articles.
Since it's such a short book, you should be able to do that during the commercials of a typical half-hour sitcom.
key_insight: Joel packages his influential web writing on technical hiring into a deliberately concise book covering the full pipeline from finding developers to fixing underperforming teams.
